title: Level 103
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:
'''The Corrupted Plain'''
}}__NOTOC__ {{Blurimage|file=Level103willbeused.jpg|align=center|caption=The last view.|width=500px}} ==
Description
== '''The Corrupted Plain''' consists of a neon green grid stretching out infinitely characterized by different elevations, linear complex shapes (e.g., a curved hole as present in fig 1.), hills, and flat planes. The climate of the level, or lack thereof, has particularly little variation, temperature ranging from 22.3°C-28.1°C, an exclusive form of "rain", being neon lines falling briefly which has been presumed to do no effect, and a barometric pressure which is not consistent (no confirmed range yet far), low or high enough to cause symptoms. Information regarding the level is limited as there are no consistent entrances or exits present. Attempts to mine into the ground have been proved mostly futile; the equipment used to mine is dented and damaged. Despite this, people have only retrieved small, crumb-sized parts of the ground. Further oversight resulted in the broken piece of ground to regenerate after 1-2 days. Primitive analysis of the retrieved material has shown that it will transform into ice if waterlogged. This ice, notably, does not melt and is hydrophobic. Throwing the material on slanted surfaces has resulted in the sample either: bouncing off the surface extremely, or absorbing into the surface. The ambience of '''The Corrupted Plain''' is characterized by "video game-like noises" and nature noises, sounds like: laser blasts, cartoon-sounding explosions, brief pixelated sound blips, rocket noises, water dripping/gushing, sounds of birds but pitch changed, and landslides can be heard reverberating throughout the level when it occurs randomly. These sounds have no source and are usually described as being far away from the listener. Curiously, wanderer-made noises do not reverberate but rather get instantly absorbed, akin to that of an anechoic chamber, although, unlike them, one does not lose their spatial awareness or suffer any effects somehow if left in the pure silence of the level. Psychotic disorders or hallucinations are not psychologically possible at this level, even despite a low mental health state. If one is about to have an episode of such, people have described the feeling to be similar to anti-depressants, feeling happiness albeit hopeless thoughts or recent pains, followed by an undertone of sadness. However, if one is about to have a hallucination, this will channel to subtle physical sensations like tingling, hypersensitivity or numbness, and muscle tension to name a few. Additionally, people without any disorders have reported emotional dysregulation, feeling emotions more intensely and the feelings could last longer. {{Blurimage|file=Level -- hills.jpg|align=center|caption=Finality in solace.|width=400px}} One must be cautious of using devices constantly, as the level undergoes brief but intense periods of electromagnetic interference (EMI), and in some rare cases, the EMI may anomalously be device-specific. Observers have reported this interference to be so extreme that their device temporarily or stops working completely after the EMI. This typically lasts 5 seconds to 2 minutes, averagely occurring twice or thrice randomly every 24 hours. Additionally, the level contains a high-functioning Wi-Fi signal, named "Plain0-4". Devices that preceding 4G can not connect to the signal, obscurely. This Wi-Fi signal is anomalously not affected by the EMI but rather becomes faster during the aftermath of the interference. There have been notable landmarks documented on '''The Corrupted Plain''', 3 being discovered so far. Analysis of these reports has shown that their position is not consistent and is teleporting, rumouring evidence of non-Euclidean properties. *'''The Mountain''' is the most common and largest landmark one can find, taking the appearance of a pyramid-shaped mountain with minimal deformations (small hills, caves/holes) and being as tall as a three-story building. It has been rumoured that the landmark bears additional features like bridges and small huts, or even other small hills alongside it, but as aforementioned, inconsistent reports have deemed this information unconfirmed. This place is the only confirmed area of the level with resources, consisting of bottles of warm water and a rope. Additionally, these resources have been observed to respawn every 2 weeks. *'''Oceanside''' takes the appearance of a flat coast, albeit the ocean is unable to be distinguished by the levels' constant grid texture. Reports have claimed that this coast can vary between infinite or small upon each new encounter. The Oceanside's liquid is safe for consumption, causing one to lose their taste briefly. This liquid can be a substitute for hydration, but there is a chance you may get long-term side effects of nausea or vertigo. One can not drown in this liquid but instead breathe in it. *'''The Hole''' is distinct from the other holes you see throughout the level, being extremely large in comparison. Estimated measures say that The Hole is about 500x500m. For example, an average hole in the level is about 50 meters (see fig 1). Attempts to go down the landmark via rope have resulted in nothing but permanent descent. Attempts to fly a drone down or drop a microphone have resulted in the audio/visual being static from EMI. {{Notice|body='''The Corrupted Plain''' does not have any exits, but has entrances that are inconsistent in nature (ranging Levels 0 to 500).}}
hello. do you know where you are? you were looking for something, i saw you throughout the freshwater abyss. you were running; aren't you ambitious? what were you running from? if you were running away from life, heh, is that your fucking coping mechanism? what you were in shall be your gift actually; being in those buzzing lights any longer would burst you! you found your way out... no wonder why people like you eventually die you ran from safety like you did back on earth look where you ended up now this level is to remind that this dimension is like some reflection of life; completely unpredictable. its like the backrooms works the same as the frontrooms, just more... amplified. you got the chance to live... you knew that. you stare at something that tells you everyone everything knows you should've been scared that the next level from where you are could've been the soft version of death oh for fuck sake... now you're crying? feel what pain? you can't handle shit? stare at the horizon; i don't give a shit if that calms you down or not. there is nothing to harm you here. you are safe
